name: GetBlock Blockchain Node API
what_it_does: Managed blockchain node provider offering shared and dedicated RPC endpoints for 50+ blockchains — enabling agents to query on-chain data, send transactions, and interact with smart contracts without running their own infrastructure.
use_cases: Querying Ethereum and EVM-compatible chain state via JSON-RPC from agent workflows
Sending signed transactions to multiple blockchains through unified endpoint management
Reading smart contract data across 50+ chains without maintaining node infrastructure
Building multi-chain agents with single API key for consistent access pattern
Prototyping Web3 applications with generous free tier before scaling
not_for: Applications requiring guaranteed request SLA (use dedicated nodes or Alchemy/Infura)
Archive node access for deep historical data (shared nodes have limitations)
Low-latency MEV or arbitrage (requires co-located dedicated nodes)
best_when: An agent needs simple JSON-RPC access to multiple blockchains with a free tier and minimal setup, without managing node infrastructure.
avoid_when: You need archive node access, guaranteed uptime SLAs, or ultra-low latency for time-sensitive trading.

auth.notes: API key passed in endpoint URL or as header. One key per project. Shared nodes use key-based rate limiting. Dashboard for key management and usage analytics.

pricing.notes: Generous free tier for development. Shared nodes have rate limits — dedicated nodes for production. Supports 50+ chains. Competitive pricing vs. Alchemy/Infura for simple use cases.

performance.rate_limits: Shared: 40K req/day free; CUPS-based on paid plans

agent_readiness.error_message_notes: Standard JSON-RPC error format. Chain-specific documentation quality varies. Dashboard shows request stats and error rates. Rate limit errors return HTTP 429 with retry guidance.
agent_readiness.idempotency_notes: Read operations (eth_call, eth_getBalance, eth_getLogs) are idempotent. Write operations (eth_sendRawTransaction) create blockchain state — not idempotent by nature.
known_agent_gotchas: Shared nodes have rate limits — 40K/day free may not be enough for production agents
No archive node on shared tier — historical data queries limited to recent blocks
Chain coverage varies — verify your target chain is supported before committing
JSON-RPC endpoint format differs per chain — check docs for chain-specific URL patterns

security_score_components.security_notes: TLS enforced on all endpoints. API key auth — treat as sensitive credential. HTTPS-only endpoints. No formal compliance certifications. Standard Web3 infrastructure security practices.
